pr.title: Melee decomp: Kirby and Yoshi fighter matches
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Exact-match decompilation work for Kirby copy ability and Yoshi special action code, mostly in ftKirby/ftkirby.c, with smaller assertion cleanup in ftkirbyspecialn.c, a Yoshi matching no-op, and removal/formatting cleanup in baselib particle code. Review focused on newly introduced pragma usage; reviewer feedback led to removing the pragma approach rather than relying on bot-cited precedent.

key_files.role: Main Kirby copy-ability matching work; refactored helper use, local/register-shaping changes, typed field access, PAD_STACK uses, split Fighter locals, and Jigglypuff hat load documentation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C, ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0
text_corpus: bot report lists ftkirby new matches and unmatched-item improvements

key_files.role: Kirby SpecialN assertion cleanup; added baselib debug include and replaced explicit OSReport plus __assert sequence with HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
Yoshi SpecialS match adjustment using a no-op expression before effect spawn.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim moving to 100.00%

key_files.role: Baselib cleanup related to pragma precedent and formatting/control-flow cleanup; removed scoped pool_data pragmas from fn_80393C14.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removes #pragma push/#pragma pool_data off/#pragma pop around fn_80393C14
review_comments: discussion about whether scoped pool_data pragmas should remain

what_changed.text: PR body claims exact-match decompilation for Kirby copy ability and Yoshi special action code, with isolated and full ship-set regression reports showing zero broken matches, zero fuzzy regressions, and zero metric regressions.

Bot report recorded new matches for ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0, ftKb_SpecialN_800F16D0, ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim, and fn_800F53AC, plus improvements for ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4, ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438, and ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C.
ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4 gained a small finish helper and separated the input index pointer into arg2_cur, preserving the original arg2 while advancing a working cursor.

ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 reused ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs instead of a repeated joint traversal setup, changed a raw byte flag write into flag_bone->flags2_b7 = true, and used indexed DObj pointer storage instead of raw byte-addressed assignment.
ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 renamed the stack buffer from sp14 to part_dobj_indices and added a comment that split Fighter* locals are required for register allocation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 comment and local changes

what_changed.text: fn_800F53AC replaced an explicit OSReport plus __assert expansion with HSD_ASSERTREPORT and removed two now-unused extern declarations.
A reviewed pragma attempt in ftkirby.c was challenged and then removed; the final loaded diff instead shows removal of #pragma pool_data off around baselib particle fn_80393C14.
evidence_refs: review_comments: fjooord possible issue/new pragmas, jurrejelle yeah remove, ribbanya remove and commit
raw_diff: particle.c removes scoped pool_data pragmas

smart_moves.text: Factored the repeated joint-reference insertion pass into ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_insert_joint_refs and reused it in ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438, reducing duplicated traversal setup in the shown diff.
Used a separate cursor variable arg2_cur while retaining the original arg2 parameter, a local-lifetime/register-shaping tactic consistent with matching work where pointer progression affects codegen.
Converted raw offset flag manipulation to a named FighterBone bitfield access where the local type made the field role clear.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ((u8*) parts2)[flag_off] |= 1 replaced by flag_bone->flags2_b7 = true

smart_moves.text: Responded to review by removing pragma-based matching rather than treating an existing bot-cited pattern as sufficient precedent.
evidence_refs: review_comments: jurrejelle says remove; ribbanya says remove and commit

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a raw byte flag access is on a known struct, prefer the struct field form if it still supports matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 uses flag_bone->flags2_b7 = true instead of byte offset write

decomp_lessons.lesson: Expanded OSReport plus __assert sequences can sometimes collapse to HSD_ASSERTREPORT when the line and report text match the local debug macro form.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftkirbyspecialn.c adds <baselib/debug.h> and replaces OSReport/__assert with HSD_ASSERTREPORT

decomp_lessons.lesson: Register-allocation matches may require split locals that point to the same Fighter object, but this should be documented narrowly rather than normalized as general style.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 adds fp and fp2 plus note that split Fighter* locals are required for register allocation

decomp_lessons.lesson: Scoped compiler pragmas are still review-sensitive even if a similar pattern exists elsewhere; reviewer feedback in this PR favored removing the pragma approach.
evidence_refs: review_comments: new pragmas challenged and removal requested
raw_diff: particle.c removes scoped pool_data pragma block

naming_conventions.name: part_dobj_indices
naming_conventions.convention: Replace opaque stack names like sp14 with a role name when the buffer stores per-part DObj indices.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 renames u8 sp14[0x90] to u8 part_dobj_indices[0x88]

naming_conventions.name: arg2_cur
naming_conventions.convention: Use a *_cur suffix for an advancing cursor derived from an input pointer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4 adds u8* arg2_cur and increments it instead of arg2

naming_conventions.name: root
naming_conventions.convention: Use root for the starting HSD_Joint pointer from hat dynamics.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 changes ftDynamics* dyn to HSD_Joint* root

naming_conventions.name: flag_bone
naming_conventions.convention: Use a local role name when a bone pointer is specifically used for flag mutation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438 adds FighterBone* flag_bone = bone

assembly_or_matching_tactics.description: PR used PAD_STACK(4) in a new finish helper and PAD_STACK(8) in Kirby hat/switch code to shape stack/register output.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Global standard says PAD_STACK needs explicit verifier evidence; PR body provides regression evidence, but no line-by-line objdiff proof is included in the slice.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split identical Fighter* locals for register allocation
assembly_or_matching_tactics.description: ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 use fp and fp2 from gobj->user_data; ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 explicitly documents the split locals as required for register allocation.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 note about split Fighter* locals
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as a narrow matching tactic, not normal source style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.description: ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim inserts (void) (fp != NULL) before efSync_Spawn, likely to influence emitted code while preserving behavior.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report shows ftYs_SpecialAirSEnd_Anim at 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a codegen tactic and should remain evidence-backed and local.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.description: Replacing OSReport plus __assert with HSD_ASSERTREPORT both cleaned source and matched fn_800F53AC according to bot report.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists fn_800F53AC as 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Only apply where file, line, message/report behavior and include surface support the macro.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Avoid or remove pool_data pragma exceptions when challenged
assembly_or_matching_tactics.description: An attempted new pragma in ftkirby.c was reviewed, debated using particle.c as precedent, and removed; final diff also removes an existing pool_data pragma block in particle.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Do not infer that all existing pool_data pragmas are banned; the PR evidence only supports that this PR's new pragma approach was not accepted.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Flagged a possible issue with new pragmas in ftkirby.c.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.feedback: Argued the scoped pool_data pragma was acceptable based on existing particle.c precedent and clean validation at head 76b0617.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Requested removal of the new pragma.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Rejected the bot-cited precedent framing and said the pragma would be removed and committed.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching ftKb_SpecialN_800EF0E4_finish or removing PAD_STACK in Kirby SpecialN.
Search for other PR review comments rejecting #pragma pool_data off or new pragmas in fighter code.
Search for HSD_ASSERTREPORT replacements of OSReport plus __assert in fighter translation units.
Search for no-op expressions like (void) (fp != NULL) used as exact-match tactics.
Check whether part_dobj_indices became a recurring name in Kirby hat loading functions.

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: claimed exact matches and regression reports
text_corpus: bot report with function-level before/after percentages
notes: The PR body claims 5 newly exact functions across 3 files, while the loaded bot issue comment shows 4 new matches and 4 improvements at that point; this likely reflects different revision timing, so function counts should be cited with their source.

Review comments reference a new pragma near ftKb_SpecialN_800EEC34, but the final loaded diff excerpt does not include that added pragma and instead shows removal of pool_data pragmas from particle.c.
Verification is reported in PR text and bot output, not independently rerun during intake.
Some matching tactics, especially PAD_STACK, split locals, and no-op expressions, are inferred from diffs and match reports; do not generalize them without additional objdiff evidence.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer typed FighterBone flag fields over raw byte flag offsets when the type is known
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftKb_SpecialN_800EF438, a raw byte write through ((u8*) parts2)[flag_off] was replaced with flag_bone->flags2_b7 = true, supporting the typed-fields-over-pointer-math standard for fighter bone flags.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use HSD_ASSERTREPORT for matching report-plus-assert expansions when supported
accepted_candidate_records.text: fn_800F53AC replaced OSReport(ftKb_Init_803CB510, ...) plus __assert(...) with HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x66, 0, ftKb_Init_803CB510), alongside adding <baselib/debug.h> and removing obsolete externs.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists fn_800F53AC as a new 100.00% match

accepted_candidate_records.title: Do not treat bot-cited precedent as enough to justify new matching pragmas
accepted_candidate_records.text: A new pragma in ftkirby.c was challenged; despite a comment arguing scoped pool_data precedent from particle.c and clean checks, reviewers requested removal and the pragma approach was removed.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify review expectations for matching pragmas
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2685 suggests that even scoped pragmas with apparent in-tree precedent can be rejected during review; accepted use should require narrow scope, explicit objdiff/regression evidence, and reviewer agreement, and bot-generated precedent should not by itself justify a new pragma.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: particle.c removes scoped pool_data pragmas
source_update_candidates.reason: This is consistent with existing standards but PR evidence may help sharpen wording around bot precedent and scoped pragma exceptions.

source_update_candidates.title: Document split duplicate locals as a narrow matching tactic
source_update_candidates.text: ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 documents that split Fighter* locals are required for register allocation. This could be added as an example under matching tactics, with a warning that it needs objdiff evidence and should be narrowly scoped.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftKb_SpecialN_800F11F0 comment and fp/fp2 locals
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete example of a register-allocation tactic already framed as exceptional by an inline note.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the claim that PR #2685 definitively added 5 exact matches without preserving the conflict between PR body and loaded bot report.
Do not promote a blanket rule banning all #pragma pool_data off; the evidence only shows this PR's new pragma was removed and one particle.c block was removed.
Do not treat PAD_STACK, split duplicate locals, or no-op casts as accepted general style; they are matching tactics requiring narrow verification.
Do not infer reviewer intent beyond the explicit pragma removal comments.
